channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Paula Abdul settles lawsuit that alleged sexual assault by 'American Idol' producer Nigel Lythgoe
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Paula Abdul and former “American Idol” producer Nigel Lythgoe have agreed to settle a lawsuit in which she alleged he sexually assaulted her in the early 2000s when she was a judge on the show.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: EPA bans cancer-causing chemicals that contaminated Camp Lejeune
item.description: Veterans tell NewsNation the prohibition of trichloroethylene and perchloroethylene is a bittersweet step forward for them.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, NewsNation

item.title: Jill Biden tells military children at Marine Corps Reserve toy drive to remember that they are loved
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Less than 10 minutes was all it took for a large pile of toys donated by the White House staff to disappear as Jill Biden and children from military families sorted them into boxes as part of the annual Marine Corps Reserve Toys for Tots campaign for the less fortunate.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Supreme Court will hear Catholic charitable group's plea to be free from Wisconsin unemployment tax
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Friday said it would take up a new religious rights case over whether a Catholic charitable organization must pay Wisconsin's employment tax.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Middle East latest: Syrians celebrate Assad's fall as US seeks a peaceful political transition
item.description: Thousands of Syrians gathered in Damascus’ main square and a historic mosque for the first Muslim Friday prayers since former President Bashar Assad was overthrown, a major symbolic moment for the country’s dramatic change of power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judge rejects an attempt by Trump campaign lawyer to invalidate guilty plea in Georgia election case
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — The judge overseeing the Georgia election interference case against Donald Trump and others on Friday rejected an attempt by former Trump campaign lawyer Kenneth Chesebro to invalidate his guilty plea.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Conan O'Brien's parents die within days of each other
item.description: Dr. Thomas O’Brien and Ruth R. O’Brien, the parents of late-night icon Conan O’Brien, both passed away within days of each other at their home in Brookline, Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: American released from Syrian prison is flown out of the country, a US official says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military has transported out of Syria an American who had disappeared seven months ago into former President Bashar Assad’s notorious prison system and was among the thousands released this week by rebels, a U.S. official said Friday.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Woman who falsely accused Duke lacrosse players of rape in 2006 publicly admits she lied
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — The woman who in 2006 falsely accused three Duke University lacrosse players of raping her – making national headlines that stirred tensions about race, class and the privilege of college athletes -- has admitted publicly for the first time that she made up the story.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: OpenAI's Altman will donate $1 million to Trump's inaugural fund
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is planning to make a $1 million personal donation to President-Elect Donald Trump's inauguration fund, joining a number of tech companies and executives who are working to improve their relationships with the incoming administration.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Nephew's Christmas revelation leads to cold-case killer's prison sentence
item.description: A convicted killer who had escaped justice for more than a decade learned Thursday that he will likely spend the rest of his life in prison.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, Fox 5

item.title: New Jersey governor wants more federal resources for probe into drone sightings
item.description: TOMS RIVER, N.J. (AP) — Gov. Phil Murphy has asked the Biden administration to put more resources into an investigation of mysterious drone sightings that have been reported in New Jersey and nearby states.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Thousands of Syrians celebrate in central Damascus during first Friday prayers since Assad's fall
item.description: DAMASCUS (AP) — Thousands of Syrians gathered Friday in Damascus' historic main mosque for the first Muslim Friday prayers since the ouster of President Bashar Assad, while giant crowds celebrated in the capital's largest square and across the country.
item.dc:creator: ALBERT AJI and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Is Friday the 13th really that unlucky? Not for Mega Millions players in Michigan
item.description: Sure, some people might tell you to be extra careful on Friday the 13th, but according to the Michigan Lottery it might be worth taking some risks.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Clarke, Local 4

item.title: Hearings are done: Now the wait begins for verdict on closely-watched climate case at UN’s top court
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — A lawyer for a small South Pacific island nation told the world’s highest court to heed her warning.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A man has pleaded guilty to the death of a 5-year-old girl living in their Kansas homeless camp
item.description: A man who lived in a makeshift camp with a homeless 5-year-old Kansas girl and her father has pleaded guilty to rape and first-degree murder in connection with the child’s death.
item.dc:creator: John Hanna And Heather Hollingsworth, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Prosecutors will seek the death penalty for 2 Venezuelan men accused of killing Texas girl
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Prosecutors in Texas announced Friday that they will seek the death penalty against two Venezuelan men who are accused of killing a 12-year-old Houston girl after they had entered the U.S. illegally.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dick Vitale says he's cancer-free after 4 bouts with disease in just over 3 years
item.description: Vitale had surgery in the summer to remove cancerous lymph nodes from his neck. He was previously treated for melanoma and lymphoma, and had radiation treatments last year for vocal cord cancer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Amazon to donate $1 million to Trump inauguration, matching Meta
item.description: E-commerce giant Amazon is planning to donate $1 million to President-elect Donald Trump’s inaugural fund, matching the amount Meta already donated to the incoming president’s inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Americans threaten to take action as drones terrorize multiple states and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nearly half of US teens are online 'constantly': Pew report
item.description: Nearly half of American teenagers say they are online “constantly” despite concerns about the effects of social media and smartphones on their mental health, according to a new report published Thursday by the Pew Research Center.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Some in seafood industry see Trump as fishermen's friend, but tariffs could make for pricier fish
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — The incoming administration of President-elect Donald Trump is likely to bring big changes for one of the oldest sectors of the U.S. economy — seafood — and some in the industry believe the returning president will be more responsive to its needs.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missouri man Travis Timmerman, missing since June, freed from Syrian prison
item.description: A Missouri man found in Syria said early Friday he was freed following the overthrow of Syrian President Bashar Assad after seven months in prison.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Hammer, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New York City mayor meets with Trump's 'border czar' to discuss how to go after 'violent' criminals
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams met with President-elect Donald Trump's incoming “border czar” on Thursday, with the Democratic mayor expressing an enthusiasm to work with the incoming administration to pursue violent criminals in the city while Trump promises mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Police say suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing wasn't a client of the insurer
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The man charged with killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson was not a client of the medical insurer and may have targeted it because of its size and influence, a senior police official said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fentanyl found inside dolphins in Gulf of Mexico
item.description: Traces of fentanyl were found inside bottlenose dolphins that were found dead in the Gulf of Mexico, raising concerns that the deadly drug may be affecting other sea life.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, Fox 5

item.title: RFK Jr.'s daughter-in-law met with Ratcliffe about CIA job: Washington Post
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s daughter-in-law took a private meeting with John Ratcliffe, President-elect Trump’s pick to run the CIA, in an apparent bid for a high-ranking position within the agency, The Washington Post reported.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, Fox 5

item.title: New Jersey, Minnesota sue Glock over switch that allows pistols to fire like machine gun
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (AP) — New Jersey and Minnesota sued Glock on Thursday, calling on the gunmaker to stop selling firearms that can be adapted with dime-sized switches to fire up to 1,200 rounds a minute.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: San Diego County votes to become 'super sanctuary' for migrants
item.description: A new San Diego County ordinance expands its status as a sanctuary county and further limits communication with ICE
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Brown snow falls over Maine town; officials warn against touching, eating it
item.description: Residents of a Maine town are being asked to avoid touching or ingesting the “brown” show that fell in the area.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, Fox 5

item.title: Box truck loses control, hits fire truck on Michigan highway, video shows
item.description: No first responders were hurt, but the driver of the box truck was taken to the hospital with minor injuries.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rosendale, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Luxury real estate broker and his twin held without bail on sex trafficking charges
item.description: A prominent luxury real estate broker and his identical twin brother appeared in a Florida courtroom Thursday on sexual assault charges. Another brother, who co-founded the real estate firm, is also charged in what federal officials call a longtime sex trafficking scheme.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Black denomination upholds stance against same-sex marriage. A gay pastor in its ranks seeks change
item.description: When the African Methodist Episcopal Church, arguably the world’s largest independent Black Protestant denomination, held its quadrennial General Conference in Ohio in August, among the agenda items was an issue that the Rev. Jennifer S. Leath had labored over for two decades: same-sex marriage.
item.dc:creator: DARREN SANDS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump again named Time's Person of the Year
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump rang the opening bell Thursday at the New York Stock Exchange after being recognized for the second time by Time magazine as its Person of the Year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump is named Time's Person of the Year, will ring the New York Stock Exchange bell
item.description: About six months ago, Donald Trump was sitting in a courtroom in lower Manhattan listening to a jury make him the first former president convicted of a crime.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and DAVID BAUDER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bill Clinton says he’s open to talking to Biden about preemptive pardon for Hillary Clinton
item.description: Former President Bill Clinton said he is open to having a conversation with President Joe Biden about a potential preemptive pardon for his wife, former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Alaska Airlines will spread its wings by flying to Tokyo and Seoul beginning next year
item.description: Alaska Airlines said Tuesday it will start new service to Tokyo and Seoul next year as part of a plan to boost international flights over the next several years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5
